title: limonium virgatum subsp. divaricatum
intro: Limonium virgatum subsp. divaricatum, also known as sea lavender or divaricate sea lavender, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae, commonly found in coastal areas and salt marshes. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Limonium virgatum subsp. divaricatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and aid in the relief of digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
